title: Automotive Glass Installers and Repairers
description: Replace or repair broken windshields and window glass in motor vehicles.

tasks.text: Prime all scratches on pinchwelds with primer and allow to dry.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove all dirt, foreign matter, and loose glass from damaged areas, apply primer along windshield or window edges, and allow primer to dry.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Allow all glass parts installed with urethane ample time to cure, taking temperature and humidity into account.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply a bead of urethane around the perimeter of each pinchweld and dress the remaining urethane on the pinchwelds so that it is of uniform level and thickness.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select appropriate tools, safety equipment, and parts, according to job requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Obtain windshields or windows for specific automobile makes and models from stock and examine them for defects prior to installation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check for and remove moisture or contamination in damaged areas and keep areas dry until repairs are complete.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Replace all moldings, clips, windshield wipers, or other parts that were removed prior to glass replacement or repair.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove broken or damaged glass windshields or window glass from motor vehicles, using hand tools to remove screws from frames holding glass.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove moldings, clips, windshield wipers, screws, bolts, and inside A-pillar moldings and lower headliners in preparation for installation or repair work.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install, repair, or replace safety glass and related materials, such as back glass heating elements, on vehicles or equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cool or warm glass in the event of temperature extremes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Replace or adjust motorized or manual window-raising mechanisms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install new foam dams on pinchwelds, if required.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install rubber channeling strips around edges of glass or frames to weatherproof windows or to prevent rattling.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Hold cut or uneven edges of glass against automated abrasive belts to shape or smooth edges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut flat safety glass according to specified patterns or perform precision pattern making and glass cutting to custom fit replacement windows.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
